commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.6.1: the trigger — IntersectionObserver as an oscilloscope (#22)

Reskin the trigger node into a CRT oscilloscope: the step events are a
phosphor pulse train on a measured graticule, the beam sweeps with scroll,
and each pulse crossing the TRIGGER LEVEL line (the rootMargin) latches its
onStep. The scope's trigger IS the IntersectionObserver's threshold.

- [LAW:one-source-of-truth] the trigger level is authored once (--sc-trig):
  it sets the dashed line's top AND the SVG pulse geometry (peak<trig<base),
  so 'beam swept past pulse i' IS 'pulse i crossed the trigger' — the fired
  latch derives from that one geometric fact, no parallel threshold to drift.
- [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow] the trace is built once from step data;
  paint only reveals it via clip-path and reads which centres the beam passed.
- Decorative phosphor flicker is a CSS @keyframes, auto-gated by the global
  prefers-reduced-motion kill. paint(p,n) writes state only.
- Establishes the Observe movement's CRT signature, shared with bind; no
  other section wears it.

Verified in Chrome across the full scroll range (armed 0/5 -> 2/5 -> 5/5),
zero console errors.
